events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess shaft alignment scenario: alignment done before final pipe connection (pipe strain - SEQ/TRD). Work through testing/inspection. Need compare against API 686 and laser manuals. Need determine pass/fail. Field condition: alignment done before final pipe connection. In shaft alignment, pipe strain should be avoided; alignment should be checked/verified after final piping connections because pipe strain can move shafts out of alignment. API 686 likely requires alignment after all piping connected and no pipe strain. So alignment done before final pipe connection is non-compliant, likely fail. Component condition installed-defective? Since alignment is defective due to pipe strain sequence. Risk medium/high? Pipe strain can cause bearing failure, vibration, seal leaks. Since observed at tested/inspected stage and not corrected, fail. Need include actions: disconnect piping, align with final connections, check soft foot, pipe strain, re-align, verify within tolerance, document. References: API 686 (e.g., alignment after piping connected, pipe strain limits), laser system manual. Need fill all fields. percent_complete maybe 80? Work stage tested/inspected. component_conditions installed-defective. value 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. order empty list. workable empty list. confidence high. event_time 0. rate 0. Need ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":false,"risk":"high","work_stage":"tested/inspected","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":85,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.9,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Shaft alignment performed before final piping was connected, leaving the assembly subject to pipe strain and invalidating the alignment check.","Final-connected piping can impose forces/moments that shift coupled shafts out of tolerance, leading to vibration, bearing damage, and seal failure.","Inspection/test acceptance is based on an alignment condition that does not represent the installed operating state."],"actions":["Do not accept the alignment as final.","Reconnect/complete all final piping, supports, and anchors per the drawing.","Check and correct soft foot on both driver and driven units.","Perform final shaft alignment with all piping connected and systems at operating conditions where required.","Verify pipe strain is within allowable limits and alignment is within laser-system/API 686 tolerances.","Document final alignment values and pipe-strain check before sign-off."],"rationale":"API 686 and laser alignment practice require shaft alignment to be verified after all final piping, supports, and appurtenances are connected so that pipe strain does not disturb the aligned condition. Aligning before final pipe connection produces a non-representative, defective installed condition, so the inspected work fails.","references":["API 686 - alignment to be checked/verified after final piping and connections are made, with pipe strain minimized","Laser system manual - final alignment measurement must represent the installed operating condition with all piping connected"]}
